title: Natural Relief for Hyperthyroidism: 9 Herbal Decoctions to Try

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Shogaols are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promote the production of thyroid- regulating hormones, and slow down the release of thyroid hormones, thus reducing symptoms of an overactive thyroid.

Gingerols are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stress and normalize thyroid function, which can help alleviate symptoms like anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism.

Shogaol is good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms like weight loss and rapid heartbeat.
Gingerol is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to lower thyroid hormone levels and reduce inflammation in the body, thus providing relief from symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been traditionally used to help manage symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The decoction made from ginger root may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the thyroid gland, which can cause symptoms like a rapid heartbeat and nervousness. Ginger contains compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help calm the thyroid gland and reduce its production of thyroid hormones. This, in turn, can help alleviate symptoms like weight loss, fatigue, and sensitivity to heat. Additionally, ginger may help soothe digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, which are common in people with hyperthyroidism. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestion, ginger decoctions may help people with hyperthyroidism feel more comfortable and manage their symptoms more effectively.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal decoctions that help with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering dried Ashwagandha roots. You'll need about a teaspoon of the dried roots for every cup of water. Place the Ashwagandha roots in a saucepan and add the cup of water, then bring it to a boil. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. As the water reduces, the decoction will become thicker and more potent. Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the Ashwagandha roots. Discard the solids and store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use, take 1-2 tablespoons of the decoction 2-3 times a day, mixed with warm water or other herbal teas. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using Ashwagandha for hyperthyroidism, as it may interact with medications or have side effects in some individuals.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can help regulate thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce thyroid hormone production by regulating the immune system and preventing the conversion of T4 to T3.

Astragaloside IV is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to balance thyroid hormone levels and reduce oxidative stress, which can alleviate symptoms such as weight loss, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat associated with this condition.

Triterpenoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce thyroid hormone production by acting as a natural antioxidant, thereby reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, palpitations, and tremors.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus decoctions may help with hyperthyroidism because it has properties that can calm the thyroid gland. This herb, also known as Huang Qi, is believed to balance the body's energy or 'qi'. When the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, it can lead to problems like a rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss. Astragalus membranaceus decoctions may help to slow down the production of thyroid hormone by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation. The decoctions may also improve the immune system, which can be weakened by hyperthyroidism. Studies have shown that Astragalus membranaceus has antioxidant properties, which can protect the body from damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone production. Additionally, the herb may help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can trigger hyperthyroidism. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Astragalus membranaceus decoctions may help to restore balance to the thyroid gland.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera decoction that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Withania somnifera root, water, and a saucepan. Begin by adding 1 teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera root to a saucepan for every 8 ounces of water you plan to use. Then, pour in the water and gently bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low and let the decoction simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds from the Withania somnifera root to infuse into the water. After the decoction has cooled, you can strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup, discarding the solids. It is recommended to consume 1-2 cups of the decoction per day, ideally 30 minutes before meals, to help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to block the conversion of cortisol to cortisone in the body, which in turn reduces the stimulatory effect of cortisol on the thyroid gland and helps to lower excessive thyroid hormone production.

Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they help lower thyroid hormone levels by reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), thus providing relief from hyperthyroid symptoms.

Triterpenoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production, alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress, and improve overall thyroid function.
Licorinic acid is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-anxiety and antioxidant properties that help to reduce stress and inflammation caused by an overactive thyroid gland.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions may help people with hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory properties. When you have hyperthyroidism, your thyroid gland makes too much thyroid hormone. This can cause inflammation in your thyroid gland and other parts of your body. The anti-inflammatory properties in Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions can reduce this inflammation, making it easier for your body to manage thyroid hormone levels. Additionally, Glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to have adaptogenic properties, which means it can help your body adapt to stress and balance your hormone levels. It can also help reduce the symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat. Some studies have also suggested that Glycyrrhiza glabra can help block the conversion of thyroid hormone into its active form, which can help lower thyroid hormone levels. However, more research is needed to confirm this effect.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera herbal decoction for hyperthyroidism, start by gathering 1-2 teaspoons of dried Withania somnifera root powder or 3-4 grams of the fresh root. If using fresh root, wash it thoroughly and chop it into small pieces. Next, boil 1 liter of water and add the Withania somnifera root powder or the chopped fresh root to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool down and then drink 1/2 cup to 1 cup of it 2-3 times a day. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you're already taking medications for hyperthyroidism.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, thereby regulating the production of thyroid hormones and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, tremors, and weight loss.
Polyphenols are good for hyperthyroidism because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms like anxiety and fatigue.

Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, thereby regulating its function and alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cynara scolymus, also known as artichoke, is a plant that has been used for centuries to help with various health issues. For people with hyperthyroidism, Cynara scolymus decoction may be beneficial. This is because it is high in compounds that may help block the absorption of iodine in the body, which can help reduce the production of thyroid hormones. The decoction also contains antioxidants and other nutrients that can help protect the body from damage caused by excess thyroid hormones. Additionally, Cynara scolymus may help lower levels of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), which is a hormone that regulates the production of thyroid hormones. Some studies have shown that drinking Cynara scolymus decoction may help reduce thyroid hormone levels and improve symptoms in people with hyperthyroidism. However, it's essential to note that more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and that people with hyperthyroidism should consult their doctor before using Cynara scolymus decoction as a treatment.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera decoction that can help with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering 2-3 teaspoons of dried Withania somnifera roots or 4-6 grams of fresh roots, and 2 cups of water. First, boil the water in a pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer. Add the dried or fresh Withania somnifera roots to the pot and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a pinch of ghee or honey to enhance its absorption. After the decoction is ready, strain it through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, and discard the solids. Let the decoction cool down before drinking it. It is recommended to drink 1-2 cups of Withania somnifera decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally in the morning and evening, and ideally 30 minutes before meals. Consult a healthcare professional before using Withania somnifera as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, as it may interact with other medications or worsen certain health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Schisandrins are good for hyperthyroidism because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety and weight loss.
Glycosides are good for hyperthyroidism because they help to balance thyroid hormones and reduce stress by increasing antioxidant activity and improving the body's ability to handle stress.

Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, tremors, and weight loss.

remedies.remedy_desc: Schisandra chinensis decoctions, a traditional Chinese herbal remedy, may help manage hyperthyroidism. The plant's roots and berries are rich in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds. Research suggests that Schisandra chinensis may help reduce the production of thyroid hormones. This is possibly due to its ability to modulate the thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels in the body. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as fatigue, anxiety, and weight loss. The herbal remedy may also help to improve liver function, as the liver plays a crucial role in metabolizing thyroid hormones. By supporting liver health and reducing thyroid hormone production, Schisandra chinensis decoctions may help to alleviate the symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera herbal decoction that helps with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering dried Withania somnifera roots and leaves, which can be found in health food stores or online. First, measure out one teaspoon of dried Withania somnifera roots and leaves, and place it in a clean glass container. Next, boil one cup of water in a pot, and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Pour the boiling water over the Withania somnifera mixture in the glass container, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the decoction using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass container, and discard the solids. Let the decoction cool down, then you can drink it as is, or add honey or lemon to taste. It's recommended to drink this decoction 2-3 times a day to help manage hyperthyroidism symptoms, such as anxiety, insomnia, and tremors. However, please note that Withania somnifera should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Avenanthramides are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production, lower body temperature, and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety and weight loss.

Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help to balance thyroid hormones by reducing the production of thyroid hormones in the body and also have antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress caused by excess thyroid hormones.

Phenolic acids are good for hyperthyroidism because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.

Fisetin is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to block the production of thyroid hormones by acting on the thyroid-activating enzyme, which can help to reduce the symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland.
Quercetin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, promoting balance and normal function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, is a natural remedy that may help with hyperthyroidism. The decoction of Avena sativa contains a compound called avenalin, which is believed to have a balancing effect on the thyroid gland. It is thought to help slow down the production of thyroid hormones, which can become overactive in hyperthyroidism. Avena sativa decoction is also a source of fiber, vitamins, and minerals, which can help regulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the body. This can lead to a decrease in symptoms such as anxiety, tremors, and rapid heartbeat, which are common in hyperthyroidism. Additionally, Avena sativa decoction may help reduce stress and promote relaxation, further alleviating symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera decoction that helps with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering 2-3 grams of dried Withania somnifera root and 2-3 grams of dried licorice root. Then, boil 1 liter of water in a pot and add the dried herbs. Let it boil for 10-15 minutes, then reduce the heat and let it simmer for another 20-30 minutes. After that, turn off the heat and let the decoction steep for 30 minutes to an hour, allowing the herbs to release their active compounds. Strain the decoction using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids. Allow the decoction to cool down before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the decoction, 2-3 times a day, for best results. You can also add honey or ginger to the decoction for better taste and added health benefits. However, consult with a healthcare professional before using this decoction, especially if you're taking any medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinidine is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of the condition, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss, by acting as an antithyroid agent and promoting a healthy balance of thyroid hormones in the body.
Quinine may be good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which are often associated with thyroid problems.
Tannins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to an overactive thyroid condition.
Quinovic acid is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps reduce thyroid hormone production by acting as an antioxidant and reducing oxidative stress in the body.

Quercetin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce thyroid inflammation, lower thyroid hormone production, and promote overall thyroid health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis is a plant-based remedy that has been used to help manage hyperthyroidism. The plant contains a compound called quinine, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland. This swelling can cause the gland to produce too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like a rapid heartbeat and nervousness. The quinine in Cinchona officinalis decoctions can also help to reduce the production of thyroid hormone, which can help to alleviate these symptoms. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, and may also help to regulate the immune system, which can become overactive in people with hyperthyroidism.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Withania somnifera herbal decoction that helps with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering high-quality Ashwagandha roots, a well-known herb in Ayurvedic medicine. Rinse the roots thoroughly in cold water to remove any dirt or impurities. Next, boil 1 teaspoon of the cleaned Ashwagandha roots in 2 cups of water for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the roots to release their beneficial compounds. After boiling, let the mixture steep for another 10-15 minutes, allowing it to cool down. Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids. The decoction can be taken 2-3 times a day, ideally between meals. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using Ashwagandha, especially if you're taking medications or have underlying health conditions. A typical dose is 1/2 to 1 teaspoon of the decoction per serving, and it's best to consume it at a consistent time each day to experience its benefits.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Baicalein may be good for hyperthyroidism because it has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production and protect the body from oxidative stress.

Baicalin is good for hyperthyroidism because it helps reduce the production of thyroid hormones and has anti-inflammatory properties that can calm down the overactive thyroid gland, making it a potential natural remedy to manage the condition.

Wogonin is good for hyperthyroidism because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce thyroid hormone production, alleviate symptoms such as weight loss, and promote overall thyroid health.

Flavonoids help reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism by decreasing thyroid hormone production, promoting relaxation, and regulating the body's metabolic rate, which in turn helps alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and other related issues.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis, also known as Chinese skullcap or Huang-qin, is a plant used in traditional Chinese medicine. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including hyperthyroidism. Hyperthyroidism is a condition where the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety.  The active compounds in Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions, such as baicalein and baicalin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and prevent the production of excess thyroid hormone. Additionally, Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions may also help regulate the immune system and reduce the autoimmune response that can cause hyperthyroidism.  Some studies suggest that Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions may also have a direct effect on the thyroid gland, reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) and thus reducing thyroid hormone production. However, more research is needed to confirm these findings and understand the full effects of Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions on hyperthyroidism.  It is essential to note that Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions should not be used as a replacement for conventional medical treatment for hyperthyroidism. Patients should consult with their healthcare provider before using Scutellaria baicalensis decoctions, especially if they are already taking medications for hyperthyroidism.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera herbal decoction that can help with hyperthyroidism, start by gathering the dried root of the herb, also known as Ashwagandha. Use one teaspoon of the dried root for every 8 ounces of water. If you're using a tea infuser, add the root to the infuser and if not, you can simply add the root to the boiling water. Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the root and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup and discard the root. You can drink this decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally on an empty stomach, to help manage symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety and fatigue. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using Withania somnifera as a treatment for hyperthyroidism, especially if you're already taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce the levels of thyroid hormones in the body by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, thereby providing relief from symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss.

Saponins are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce thyroid hormone production by preventing the absorption of thyroid hormones in the gut, thereby alleviating symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss associated with this condition.

Withanolides are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce thyroid hormone levels and promote relaxation by acting as a natural antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that calms the thyroid gland and prevents its overproduction of hormones.

Alkaloids, also known as Ashwagandha, are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce cortisol levels and alleviate thyroid-related stress, thereby balancing thyroid hormone production and regulating metabolism.

Triterpenoids are good for hyperthyroidism because they help reduce thyroid hormone levels by inhibiting the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase, which is involved in the production of thyroid hormones, thus providing relief from the symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera, also known as ashwagandha, is a plant used in traditional Indian medicine. The roots of this plant are used to make decoctions, or infusions, which are good for hyperthyroidism. This condition occurs when the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like weight loss, anxiety, and a rapid heartbeat. Ashwagandha decoctions may help reduce thyroid hormone production by decreasing stress and anxiety levels in the body. When we're under stress, our thyroid gland produces more hormones to help us cope. Ashwagandha has been shown to reduce cortisol, a hormone released in response to stress, which in turn may reduce thyroid hormone production. Additionally, ashwagandha has antioxidant properties that may help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. Some studies have also shown that ashwagandha may help lower thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels, which can help regulate thyroid function.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Withania somnifera herbal decoction that helps with hyperthyroidism, first, you'll need some dried Withania somnifera roots, also known as ashwagandha roots, and a clean pot. Fill the pot with four to six cups of water and add one to two teaspoons of dried ashwagandha roots. Bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for about ten to fifteen minutes. Next, let it steep for another fifteen to twenty minutes, allowing the roots to release their active ingredients into the water. Strain the liquid and discard the solids. You can add a sweetener like honey or sugar to taste if needed. Finally, drink the decoction, ideally two to three times a day, to help manage the symptoms of hyperthyroidism, such as anxiety, fatigue, and weight loss. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're currently taking medications or have any underlying medical conditions.

intro_desc: Hyperthyroidism is a condition where the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, leading to symptoms like rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. To manage this condition, herbal decoctions can be a helpful complementary therapy. A combination of herbs like Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Astragalus membranaceus can be beneficial in reducing inflammation and regulating thyroid function. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms like digestive issues and joint pain. It also has a regulating effect on thyroid hormones, which can help bring down excessive production. Astragalus membranaceus, on the other hand, is an adaptogenic herb that helps the body adapt to stress, which can contribute to hyperthyroidism. It also has a balancing effect on the immune system and can help reduce inflammation.  Fixing hyperthyroidism is crucial because it can lead to serious health problems like heart problems, osteoporosis, and eye problems if left untreated. Untreated hyperthyroidism can also cause anxiety, depression, and insomnia, which can negatively impact daily life. However, with proper management, it's possible to improve the quality of life. By using herbal decoctions like those containing ginger and astragalus, individuals can alleviate symptoms and regulate their thyroid function. This can help them regain energy, improve mood, and enjoy daily activities without feeling anxious or exhausted.
supplementary_best: To remedy hyperthyroidism, a combination of herbal decoctions is needed. The best combination includes Zingiber officinale (ginger), Scutellaria baicalensis (Baikal skullcap), and Avena sativa (oat). Zingiber officinale helps to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms like anxiety and weight loss. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling in the thyroid gland. Scutellaria baicalensis has antioxidant properties that protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by excessive hormone production. It also helps to reduce inflammation and improve the immune system's function. Avena sativa helps to calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress. It also supports the adrenal glands, which are often overactive in people with hyperthyroidism.  The combination of these three herbs is effective because they target different aspects of hyperthyroidism. Zingiber officinale addresses the thyroid hormone production, Scutellaria baicalensis protects the thyroid gland, and Avena sativa calms the nervous system and supports the adrenal glands. This combination is a more holistic approach to treating hyperthyroidism, addressing multiple symptoms and causes rather than just one.
supplementary_avoid: If you have hyperthyroidism, you should avoid some herbal decoctions that can worsen your condition. One such herb is 'Kava' (Piper methysticum), which contains compounds that can stimulate the thyroid gland and increase thyroid hormone production. This can exacerbate hyperthyroidism symptoms like rapid heartbeat, anxiety, and weight loss.  Another herb to avoid is 'Black cohosh' (Actaea racemosa), which has been linked to thyroid problems in some studies. It may increase the risk of thyroid inflammation and damage the gland.  'Bladderwrack' (Fucus vesiculosus) and 'Dulse seaweed' (Palmaria palmata) also contain iodine, which can stimulate the thyroid gland and worsen hyperthyroidism. Excessive iodine consumption can lead to thyroid overactivity and worsen symptoms like rapid heartbeat and tremors.  'Ephedra' (Ephedra sinica) is another herb to avoid, as it contains compounds that can increase thyroid hormone production and worsen hyperthyroidism symptoms like anxiety and rapid heartbeat.  It's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal decoctions, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition like hyperthyroidism.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with hyperthyroidism, certain natural remedies can help reduce symptoms. Some people find that taking iodine supplements can be beneficial. Iodine is a mineral that helps the thyroid gland produce hormones. When you have too much iodine, it can actually make your condition worse, so it's essential to talk to a doctor before taking any supplements. Ashwagandha is another natural remedy that has been studied for its potential to help with hyperthyroidism. This herb is known to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be helpful since hyperthyroidism can cause feelings of anxiety and jitteriness. Some people also find that eating foods that are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, such as salmon, can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can be associated with hyperthyroidism. In addition, some people find that taking probiotics can help balance the gut bacteria, which is linked to the immune system and can affect thyroid function. Apple cider vinegar has also been studied for its potential to help with hyperthyroidism. The acidity in the vinegar may help slow down the production of thyroid hormones, which can be beneficial for people with an overactive thyroid.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with hyperthyroidism often experience other health issues. One common problem is anxiety or nervousness. This is because the thyroid gland produces hormones that help regulate the body's nervous system. When the thyroid gland produces too much thyroid hormone, it can lead to an overactive nervous system. The medicinal herb ashwagandha can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. Ashwagandha is an adaptogen, which means it helps the body adapt to stress. It works by balancing the production of thyroid hormones and reducing inflammation in the thyroid gland.  Some people with hyperthyroidism also experience insomnia or difficulty sleeping. This can be due to the increased metabolism caused by the excess thyroid hormone. The herb valerian root can help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. Valerian root has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep.  People with hyperthyroidism may also experience weight loss or muscle weakness. This is because the excess thyroid hormone can break down muscle tissue and increase metabolism. The herb astragalus can help support muscle health and reduce inflammation. Astragalus is an adaptogen that can help balance the production of thyroid hormones and support the immune system.  Another common issue with hyperthyroidism is an irregular heartbeat or palpitations. This can be caused by the increased heart rate and blood pressure caused by the excess thyroid hormone. The herb hawthorn can help support heart health and reduce inflammation. Hawthorn has been shown to lower blood pressure and slow down the heart rate, which can help reduce palpitations.  Finally, people with hyperthyroidism may experience brittle hair or nails, or thinning hair. This is because the excess thyroid hormone can cause an imbalance of hormones that regulate hair growth. The herb saw palmetto can help support hair growth and reduce inflammation. Saw palmetto has been shown to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hair loss.  Note: Before using any medicinal herbs, it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you are already taking medications or have a pre-existing medical condition.
